      "title": "Device limit check-then-act race condition",
      "description": "In CreateOrUpdateDevice(), the device count is checked at line 105, then insertion happens later. Between these operations, another concurrent request can insert a device, bypassing the limit. The count check uses a time window that may not capture recently-inserted devices if the window is too narrow.",
      "suggestion": "Move the device count check to immediately before the INSERT statement within the same database transaction, or use a database-level constraint (unique index with WHERE clause) to enforce the limit atomically."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "logic error",
      "file": "pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/anonstore/database.go",
      "line": 79,
      "title": "updateDevice() returns wrong error on zero rows affected",
      "description": "When updateDevice() finds no matching rows (rowsAffected == 0), it returns ErrDeviceLimitReached. However, zero rows affected could mean the device doesn't exist or is outside the time window, not necessarily that the device limit was reached. This conflates two different failure modes and will report misleading errors to callers.",
      "suggestion": "Return a distinct error (e.g., ErrDeviceNotFound or ErrDeviceExpired) when no rows are affected. Only ErrDeviceLimitReached should be returned when the device count truly exceeds the limit."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "time window precision",
      "file": "pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/anonstore/database.go",
      "line": 72,
      "title": "Inconsistent time windows across CountDevices and updateDevice",
      "description": "CountDevices is called with a 30-day lookback window (line 106), but updateDevice() uses a much narrower window: UpdatedAt ± 1 minute (line 75). This mismatch means the count check and update check operate on different device sets, creating inconsistent behavior where devices counted in the limit may not be updatable.",
      "suggestion": "Use consistent time windows across both operations, or pass the time window as a parameter so both methods use the same bounds."
    }
  ]
